identity.project_summary: An automated Python/R spatial statistics software framework designed for quantitative profiling of the tumor microenvironment (TME) in large whole-slide multiplexed images (such as t-CycIF). Features include immune residency identification (tumor vs stroma local context classification parallelized down to 2 minutes), tumor-stroma interface (TSI) contour mapping and continuous distance metrics, nearest neighbor distance (NND), cluster density, and Gini diversity. Incorporates advanced distance-based network modeling (Delaunay triangulation with distance cutoffs) to map single-cell and multi-cell type immune hubs (co-aggregates of up to 5 immune cell types), analyzing hub interaction and repulsion networks using PySAL, NetworkX, Rtree, and KDTree. Serves as the primary spatial statistics engine for Projects 12 and 9, with benchmarking validated on public CODEX and MIBI-TOF datasets. Available at Kkkzq/SPACEstat.
